sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Treasury withdrawal to reimburse the lost deposit for the Ikigai Info governance action and compensate alleged lost staking rewards.
Recipient: the Ikigai Info governance action submitter
Deliverables: On-chain distribution of funds to the intended recipient upon enactment
Deadline/expiry: instantly performed as part of the governance action's enactment on-chain

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, medium materiality): The proposal states that an Info governance action titled "Cardanoの生きがい - Ikigai -" was submitted in September 2024 shortly after the Chang hard fork.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, low materiality): The proposer says the Ikigai action was symbolic and asked the community to agree with a statement thanking contributors and expressing hope for Cardano's future.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposer claims a Cardano node bug allowed an unregistered stake key to be used, preventing the submitter from recovering a 100,000 ADA governance action deposit.

Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The proposal claims there was significant community sentiment at the time that the deposit should be reimbursed because the submitter was an early pioneer of Cardano governance.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The proposer states that entity-based budget submissions did not include this reimbursement after treasury withdrawal capability became available following the Plomin hard fork.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The proposal states that the Cardano in Oceania initiative included the reimbursement in its budget Info governance action, but that action was not approved.
missing: Independent evidence for: The proposer claims a Cardano node bug allowed an unregistered stake key to be used, preventing the submitter from recovering a 100,000 ADA governance action deposit.

Independent evidence for: The proposal claims there was significant community sentiment at the time that the deposit should be reimbursed because the submitter was an early pioneer of Cardano governance.

Independent evidence for: The proposer states that entity-based budget submissions did not include this reimbursement after treasury withdrawal capability became available following the Plomin hard fork.
Independent evidence for: The proposal states that the Cardano in Oceania initiative included the reimbursement in its budget Info governance action, but that action was not approved.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: unknown (stale snapshot)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal states that an Info governance action titled "Cardanoの生きがい - Ikigai -" was submitted in September 2024 shortly after the Chang hard fork." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The proposer claims a Cardano node bug allowed an unregistered stake key to be used, preventing the submitter from recovering a 100,000 ADA governance action deposit." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
